mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month trend in retail sales signals household demand momentum that lifts expected corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month RSAFS trends are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in less than 50% of walk-forward observations or Rank IC is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation crosses above a high threshold while FEDFUNDS remains near zero, investors price future tightening and lower equity duration values, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 12-month inflation crosses above 4% while FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes have median 20-day S&P 500 returns above zero or if returns are not lower than non-triggered periods at t-test p <= 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A falling unemployment rate confirmed by payroll expansion signals durable income growth and lower recession risk, causing investors to raise earnings expectations and producing positive 60-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses below 5.5% and PAYEMS 1-month change is positive
failure_condition: Reject if triggered labor-recovery observations produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in less than 55% of cases or if the average triggered return is below the unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production crossing back above its 2017 benchmark signals normalization of real output and supply capacity, supporting cyclical earnings and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO crossings above 100 are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of cases or if the signal coefficient is not positive in walk-forward regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts signals weakening rate-sensitive residential demand and future construction-linked earnings pressure, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below its trailing 6-month average by more than 10%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered HOUST slowdowns are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of observations or if mean triggered returns exceed non-triggered returns.

mechanism_narrative: A persistently low federal funds rate reduces discount rates and supports equity risk appetite, producing positive short-horizon S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS below 0.25% is associated with negative average 5-day S&P 500 returns or if hit rate for positive 5-day returns is below the unconditional hit rate.

mechanism_narrative: A large payroll gain while unemployment remains above full-employment levels signals that the recovery has room to continue without immediate labor overheating, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 1-month change crosses above 750 while UNRATE remains above 5%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered payroll-strength episodes have negative mean 20-day S&P 500 returns or if the triggered hit rate is not at least 5 percentage points above baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real GDP growth combined with flat industrial production and rising CPI signals demand running ahead of supply, compressing margins and raising policy-risk premia, producing negative 60-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 latest quarterly annualized growth exceeds 4%, INDPRO 3-month change is nonpositive, and CPIAUCSL 12-month inflation is above 3%
failure_condition: Reject if this demand-supply imbalance trigger is followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if its average return is not below the unconditional average.
